rule: Ground all extracted modules, entities, flows, and APIs in the provided Basic Design.
Use UI Design input as interaction evidence when present; mark it as missing when absent.
Mark assumptions explicitly instead of inventing missing requirements.
Preserve module boundaries and API standards from write-master-design when available.
Use retrieved reviewed DD samples as reference patterns, not as source requirements.
Generate DD using the detail-design Screen, API, and Batch templates.
Reuse common components for API envelopes, audit logging, pagination, upload, validation, and state handling.
Keep each DD section traceable to BD analytics, UI signals, retrieved samples, or explicit assumptions.
Review generated DD against rule-based checklists before designer approval/export.
When a required checklist rule fails, return actionable feedback for the next generation iteration.
